channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Asian Markets Surge as Trump Signals Iran De-escalation; Oil Steady
item.description: Asian markets surged on Tuesday, reversing prior losses, as U.S. President Trump signaled a potential pause in military action against Iran. The MSCI Asia ex Japan index climbed 1.3%, Japan's Nikkei 225 gained over 2%, and Australian equities rose modestly. Oil prices stabilized after a sharp decline, with Brent crude trading around $100.94 a barrel. Bond yields steadied as investor expectations for aggressive central bank rate hikes were scaled back. The U.S. dollar weakened against major currencies.

item.title: Jindal Steel Aims for Recovery Amid Price Rebound; Federal Bank Hits New Growth Marks
item.description: Jindal Steel & Power (JSL) reported a decline in Q3FY26 EBITDA due to lower realizations, but strong volume growth and upcoming capacity expansions, alongside rising steel prices, point to a recovery path. Federal Bank (FedBank) continues its growth momentum, benefiting from a better loan mix, improved profitability, and strong asset quality, reinforcing its appeal for a sustained re-rating.

item.title: India Mandates Solar Wafers: Boosts Local Production, Raises Cost Questions
item.description: India's solar sector is set for major changes as the government mandates domestic wafer production starting June 2028. Extending the ALMM rules to wafers, cells, and modules aims to build a complete manufacturing chain and reduce import reliance. This policy is expected to speed up industry consolidation, helping companies that control more production steps. However, it also brings big challenges in meeting global prices and building enough factories on time, making operations tricky for firms like Waaree Energies.

item.title: India's Digital Infrastructure: Stocks Diverge Amid Growth and Losses
item.description: India's digital infrastructure expansion continues, fueled by 5G and data demand. While Indus Towers offers stable rental income with a strong market position, Sterlite Technologies navigates a fiber recovery facing margin pressures. Tejas Networks grapples with losses and execution delays despite a high-risk growth strategy. RailTel Corporation focuses on monetizing railway assets, balancing steady revenue with lower-margin project work. This diverse operational landscape creates significant differentiation in risk and reward for investors.

item.title: Pranos Fusion Raises $6.8M to Target 'First Plasma' by 2026
item.description: Deep-tech firm Pranos Fusion has secured $6.8 million in early-stage funding, co-led by pi Ventures and Ankur Capital. The funding will speed up its fusion technology development, targeting "first plasma" by 2026. This capital supports building its tokamak, refining magnet systems, and growing its team, boosting India's standing in the global fusion race.

item.title: India's Economy Booms, Faces Energy Price Shocks, MSME Strain
item.description: India's economy is projected for robust growth (7.6% FY26, over 7% FY27), powered by exports and domestic demand. While energy security measures and the BHAVYA manufacturing scheme aim to bolster growth, geopolitical tensions are driving up energy prices. This rise threatens inflation, the rupee, and critically, India's MSMEs, which face risks from higher costs and payment delays.

item.title: Private Credit Faces Scrutiny Over Opacity, AI Risks
item.description: The U.S. private credit market, worth over $1.3 trillion, is facing intense scrutiny. Leaders warn of systemic risks from its lack of transparency and complex structures using insurance and offshore entities. Investor caution is rising, seen in fund outflows and stock drops for asset managers. Concerns are amplified by AI's impact on software companies and echoes of past financial crises.

item.title: Pranos Fusion Secures $6.8M to Build Compact Fusion Tech, Targets 2026 Plasma
item.description: Pranos Fusion, a Bengaluru-based company, has secured $6.8 million in early funding co-led by Pi Ventures and Ankur Capital. The investment will drive development of its compact magnetic fusion technology, aiming to achieve 'first plasma' by 2026. This funding supports key milestones like commissioning a tokamak, fueling a bet on fusion's role in meeting future global energy needs driven by AI and advanced manufacturing, while acknowledging fusion's high capital and long development paths.
